title: Server-side domain whitelist enforcement on all protected API routes
description:
## Why
The @agenticdream.com domain check only exists in the client-side ClientProvider (clients.context.tsx:31). Middleware and API routes never validate the email domain. A user with a valid Supabase JWT from any domain can call internal API routes.

## IMPORTANT: Candidate routes must stay unauthenticated
Candidates (interviewees) access the product WITHOUT any login. These routes must never have auth applied:
- Pages: /call, /result
- API: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations and all sub-paths

## What to change
1. In src/middleware.ts: for non-public routes, after confirming session exists, also check session.user.email ends with @agenticdream.com — if not, treat as unauthenticated (redirect to /sign-in for pages, 401 for API).
2. For internal-only API routes (routes NOT in the publicRoutes list above), add a server-side helper call to verify @agenticdream.com. Internal routes include: /api/user, /api/create-interview, /api/delete-interview, /api/toggle-interview-status, /api/interviews, /api/interviewers, /api/create-interviewer, /api/generate-insights, /api/generate-interview-questions, /api/interview-jobs, /api/responses, /api/responses/emails, /api/get-dreamit-jobs, /api/analyze-communication, /api/feedback.
3. DO NOT add auth to: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations, /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording — these are called during candidate interview sessions.

## Edge cases
- Candidate accessing /call with no cookies: works as before, no auth required
- Internal user with valid @agenticdream.com JWT: no change
- External Google account trying to access /dashboard or internal API: 401/redirect
- Candidate hitting /api/deepgram-token during interview: must not require auth

## How to verify
1. Access /call without any session cookie — EXPECT: page loads normally
2. Access /dashboard without any session cookie — EXPECT: redirect to /sign-in
3. Call /api/register-call without auth — EXPECT: 200 (candidate flow works)
4. Call /api/interviews without auth — EXPECT: 401
5. Call /api/interviews with non-@agenticdream.com JWT — EXPECT: 401

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a security-conscious operator, I want all protected server-side routes and middleware to enforce the @agenticdream.com domain whitelist, so that a valid Supabase JWT from any other domain cannot access user data or protected pages.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Shared server-side helper:
   - Create (or extend) a helper such as src/lib/get-auth-session.ts that: retrieves the current Supabase session, checks that session.user.email ends with '@agenticdream.com', and returns null (not the session) if either condition fails.
   - The helper must be usable by both middleware and individual API route handlers without code duplication.

2. Middleware enforcement — src/middleware.ts:
   - Replace any existing session check with the new helper.
   - A session with a non-@agenticdream.com email must be treated identically to a missing session: user is unauthenticated, redirect to /sign-in.
   - A missing session (first visit, no cookies) must continue to reach public routes without error.

3. API route enforcement — every route under src/app/api/ that handles authenticated user data (including at minimum /api/user, /api/get-call, /api/register-call):
   - Call the shared helper on each request.
   - Return HTTP 401 (not 403, not 302) if the helper returns null.
   - Must not leak session data or user records in the error body.

Edge cases:
- First-visit / no cookies: helper returns null; middleware allows public routes unaffected; API routes return 401 (same as any unauthenticated call).
- Valid @agenticdream.com JWT: no behavior change — all existing routes continue to work.
- External Google account with a valid Supabase JWT (the bypass scenario): middleware redirects to /sign-in; API routes return 401.
- JWT present but expired (separate from domain check): handled upstream by getSession returning null — helper must not crash on null session.
- Case sensitivity: email check must be case-insensitive (@AGENTICDREAM.COM must also pass).

How to verify:
1. Obtain (or fabricate in a test) a valid Supabase JWT for a non-@agenticdream.com email (e.g. attacker@gmail.com).
2. Send a request to /api/get-call (or any protected API route) with that JWT in the Authorization/Cookie header.
3. EXPECT: HTTP 401 response. No user data in the body.
4. Attempt to navigate to /dashboard with that JWT in session cookies.
5. EXPECT: redirect to /sign-in, not the dashboard.
6. Repeat steps 2-5 with a valid @agenticdream.com JWT.
7. EXPECT: normal 200 / dashboard load — no regression.

Do NOT add auth to: /api/register-call, /api/check-response, /api/get-call, /api/fn, /api/invitations (and sub-paths), /api/deepgram-token, /api/upload-recording. Only apply the @agenticdream.com check to these routes: /api/user, /api/create-interview, /api/delete-interview, /api/toggle-interview-status, /api/interviews, /api/interviewers, /api/create-interviewer, /api/generate-insights, /api/generate-interview-questions, /api/interview-jobs, /api/responses, /api/responses/emails, /api/get-dreamit-jobs, /api/analyze-communication, /api/feedback.

title: Remove leftover /call/[id] route causing Next.js slug conflict
description:
## Why
Build is broken. Next.js throws: 'You cannot use different slug names for the same dynamic path (id !== interviewId)'. The [id] directory was not deleted when [interviewId] was added, leaving two conflicting dynamic segments at the same level.

## What to change
- Delete src/app/(user)/call/[id]/ and everything inside it (only file is page.tsx)
- Grep the codebase for any remaining hrefs, redirects, or router.push calls referencing /call/[id] or params.id in the call context and update them to use /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]

## Edge cases
- Make sure no other dynamic route at the same level as [interviewId] exists after deletion
- If [id]/page.tsx had logic not carried over to [interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx, port it before deleting

## How to verify
- Run: yarn build — it MUST pass locally before committing. Do not commit until build is green.
- No src/app/(user)/call/[id] directory exists
- git status is clean
- git push succeeds

comments.author: eng1

Fix: create [interviewId]/page.tsx (1-seg) from [id]/page.tsx (params.id→params.interviewId), then git rm [id]/. Resolver redirect /call/${invitation.id} will now hit [interviewId] 1-seg correctly. No href updates needed — existing /call/${slug} URLs already match the renamed segment. Run yarn build to confirm. Files: src/app/(user)/call/[id]/page.tsx → [interviewId]/page.tsx. Test: yarn build must exit 0.

title: Candidate identity verification — require email entry and validate against invitation
description: Currently the call page pre-fills the candidate's email and name from the invitation record, but those fields are editable — anyone with the invitation URL can submit under any identity. The fix: remove pre-fill, prompt the candidate to enter their own email and name, and validate the entered email server-side against the invitation's candidate_email before allowing the interview to proceed or a response to be created. This ensures only the invited person can take the interview.

title: ClientProvider fetch /api/user does not check response status before parsing JSON
description:
## Why
clients.context.tsx line 37-38: fetch('/api/user') result is immediately .json()'d without checking res.ok. If /api/user returns 500 (DB error), the body is { error: '...' } — an object. The check 'if (!userData)' passes (truthy object) and setClient() is called with an error object as the user, silently corrupting client state.

## What to change
File: src/contexts/clients.context.tsx, after the fetch call:

1. Check res.ok (or res.status < 400) before calling .json()
2. If !res.ok: call signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed') — same as user-not-found path
3. Wrap the entire fetch block in try/catch: on network error, call signOut() and redirect

Also wrap the entire handleAuthUser function body in try/catch so any unexpected error triggers signOut + redirect rather than silently crashing the component.

## Edge cases
- 200 with valid user: no change
- 200 with null (user not found): existing behavior preserved
- 500 DB error: now correctly signs out and redirects instead of setting corrupt client state
- Network failure (fetch throws): caught, user is signed out cleanly

## How to verify
- Mock /api/user to return 500
- Sign in with valid @agenticdream.com account
- EXPECT: clean redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed, no corrupt client state

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a signed-in user, I want the app to handle API errors gracefully when loading my profile, so that a server-side failure never silently corrupts my client session with an error object.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Response status check — src/contexts/clients.context.tsx after the fetch('/api/user') call:
   - Before calling .json(), check res.ok (or res.status < 400).
   - If !res.ok: call supabase.auth.signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'). Do not call .json() or setClient() on this path.

2. Network error handling:
   - Wrap the fetch call (and .json() parse) in a try/catch.
   - On any thrown error (network failure, JSON parse failure): call supabase.auth.signOut() and router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed').

3. Full function guard:
   - Wrap the entire handleAuthUser function body in a top-level try/catch.
   - Any unexpected error that escapes inner handlers must also result in signOut + redirect, not a silent crash.

4. Existing paths preserved:
   - 200 with valid user object: no change — setClient() called as before.
   - 200 with null/undefined (user not found): existing signOut + redirect behavior preserved.

Edge cases:
- 500 DB error returning { error: '...' } body: now correctly redirects; must NOT call setClient() with the error object.
- 401 from domain-whitelist enforcement (zfz.1): treated as !res.ok — signOut + redirect.
- Network timeout or DNS failure (fetch throws): caught by try/catch — signOut + redirect.
- JSON parse failure on a malformed 200 response: caught by try/catch — signOut + redirect.
- User navigates away before fetch resolves: must not call setState or router.push after unmount.

How to verify:
1. Temporarily make /api/user return HTTP 500 with body { error: 'db_error' }.
2. Sign in with a valid @agenticdream.com account and complete the OAuth flow.
3. EXPECT: browser redirects to /sign-in?error=auth_failed. No corrupt client state. DevTools Network shows the 500, not a crash.
4. Restore /api/user to normal. Sign in again.
5. EXPECT: dashboard loads normally — no regression.

comments.author: eng2
comments.text: PLAN: Fix fetch /api/user missing res.ok check in src/contexts/clients.context.tsx. 1. Add isMounted flag to useEffect to guard setState/router.push after unmount. 2. Wrap entire handleAuthUser body in outer try/catch (signOut + redirect on escape). 3. Wrap fetch + .json() in inner try/catch: on network/parse error, signOut + router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'). 4. Add res.ok check before .json(): if !res.ok, signOut + router.push('/sign-in?error=auth_failed'), return. 5. Preserve existing 200-null path (signOut + unauthorized redirect) and 200-valid-user path (setClient). 6. Guard setClient and router.push calls with isMounted. Files: src/contexts/clients.context.tsx. Test: yarn build (no test files).

title: Navbar getSession missing error handling and refresh_token_not_found guard
description:
## Why
src/components/navbar.tsx calls supabase.auth.getSession() but never destructures the error field. If the refresh token is stale (refresh_token_not_found), the navbar silently proceeds with a null session but shows no error and doesn't clear the cookie — user sees a broken logged-in state until they take an action.

## What to change
File: src/components/navbar.tsx, around lines 38-42

1. Destructure error from getSession():
   const { data: { session }, error } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
2. On error.code === 'refresh_token_not_found': call signOut(), setUser(null), setLoading(false) and return
3. On any other error: setUser(null), setLoading(false) and return — log the error

## Edge cases
- Normal session: no change
- Expired token (refresh_token_not_found): clean sign-out, navbar shows logged-out state
- Other session errors: navbar shows logged-out state, no crash

## How to verify
- Set a garbage sb-refresh-token cookie, load any page with navbar
- EXPECT: navbar shows sign-in link, no console errors about undefined session

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a user with a stale refresh token, I want the navbar to detect the invalid session and show me the logged-out state immediately, so that I am not stuck seeing a broken half-authenticated UI.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Error destructuring — src/components/navbar.tsx ~line 38:
   - Change the getSession call to: const { data: { session }, error } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
   - The error variable must be checked before using session.

2. refresh_token_not_found handling:
   - If error exists AND error.code === 'refresh_token_not_found': call supabase.auth.signOut(), set user to null, set loading to false, and return early.
   - No further processing must occur on this path.

3. Any other error handling:
   - If error exists for any other reason: set user to null, set loading to false, log the error (e.g. console.error), and return early.
   - Must not throw or crash the component.

4. Happy path unchanged:
   - If no error and session is non-null: existing behavior preserved exactly.
   - If no error and session is null (logged-out user): existing behavior preserved exactly.

Edge cases:
- Normal authenticated session: no change in behavior.
- Expired/revoked token (refresh_token_not_found): navbar shows sign-in link/state, no console error flood, stale cookie is cleared via signOut().
- Network error or other AuthApiError: navbar shows signed-out state, error logged once, no crash.
- Component unmounts before getSession resolves: must not call setState after unmount (guard with isMounted flag or cleanup).

How to verify:
1. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies. Set the Supabase refresh token cookie to a garbage value.
2. Load any page that includes the navbar.
3. EXPECT: navbar renders in logged-out state (shows sign-in link). No repeated AuthApiError in console. No broken UI showing a partial logged-in state.
4. Clear the garbage cookie and reload.
5. EXPECT: navbar renders normally (no regression for valid or missing session).

title: Auth flow hardening — eliminate all remaining login edge cases
description: Comprehensive fix for all remaining gaps in the Google OAuth / Supabase auth flow identified during a full audit after the stale refresh token bug (agentfoloup-s5r). Goals: no silent auth failures, proper server-side security enforcement, and clear user-facing error messages for every failure path.

title: Google OAuth redirect loop caused by stale refresh token
description:
## Why this issue exists
When a user has an invalid or expired Supabase refresh token stored in their browser cookies, supabase.auth.getSession() tries to silently refresh it, gets back AuthApiError: Invalid Refresh Token: Refresh Token Not Found (400), and returns a null session. The middleware then treats the user as unauthenticated and redirects them to /sign-in. When the user clicks 'Continue with Google', the OAuth flow starts but the stale token cookies are never cleared, so after exchangeCodeForSession sets new tokens, the old invalid cookies interfere — or the session cookie is not readable by middleware on the redirect — and the user lands back on /sign-in again.

## Error observed
[AuthApiError: Invalid Refresh Token: Refresh Token Not Found] { status: 400, code: 'refresh_token_not_found' }

## Affected files (from investigation)
- src/app/auth/callback/route.ts — exchanges OAuth code for session
- src/middleware.ts — session guard that redirects to /sign-in
- src/contexts/clients.context.tsx — calls getSession() / onAuthStateChange, may emit the error

## What needs to change
1. In clients.context.tsx (and anywhere getSession is awaited): catch efresh_token_not_found errors and call supabase.auth.signOut() to fully clear stale cookies, then redirect to /sign-in for a fresh login.
2. In src/app/auth/callback/route.ts: after exchangeCodeForSession, verify the session was actually created; if it failed or is null, redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed instead of silently proceeding.
3. In src/middleware.ts: on any session-read error (not just missing session), clear cookies and redirect cleanly to /sign-in rather than looping.

## Edge cases
- User with no cookies at all (first visit) — should not be affected
- User whose token expired naturally vs. token that was revoked — both should get clean sign-out + redirect
- Non-@agenticdream.com Google accounts — still get blocked by domain whitelist (intentional)

## How to verify
1. Manually set an invalid sb-refresh-token cookie in DevTools
2. Navigate to /dashboard
3. Expect: clean redirect to /sign-in (no loop, no console error flood)
4. Click 'Continue with Google', complete auth
5. Expect: land on /dashboard with valid session and no errors in server logs

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a returning user with a stale/revoked Google OAuth session, I want the app to cleanly clear my invalid token and let me log in again, so that I am never stuck in a redirect loop between /sign-in and /dashboard.

Acceptance criteria:

1. Stale token detection — clients.context.tsx (and any other call site that awaits getSession):
   - On AuthApiError with code 'refresh_token_not_found', call supabase.auth.signOut() to purge all session cookies, then redirect to /sign-in.
   - Must NOT loop: after signOut the redirect to /sign-in must be a terminal action, not retriggered by the same error.

2. OAuth callback hardening — src/app/auth/callback/route.ts:
   - After exchangeCodeForSession, check that session is non-null before proceeding.
   - If session is null or the exchange errored, redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed (not silently forward the user).

3. Middleware cleanup — src/middleware.ts:
   - On any session-read error (not only missing session), clear auth cookies and redirect cleanly to /sign-in.
   - Must not issue a redirect that causes the middleware to re-evaluate and loop.

Edge cases:
- First-visit user (no cookies) — no change in behavior; must not trigger signOut path.
- Naturally expired token vs. revoked token — both treated identically: signOut + clean redirect.
- Non-@agenticdream.com Google account — domain whitelist logic is unaffected; still blocked as before after a clean auth exchange.
- Race condition: user has two tabs open; signOut in one tab should not cause infinite-loop behavior in the other.
- exchangeCodeForSession network failure (transient) — should redirect to /sign-in?error=auth_failed, not crash or loop.

How to verify:
1. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies. Set sb-refresh-token (or equivalent Supabase cookie) to a garbage value.
2. Navigate to /dashboard.
3. EXPECT: single clean redirect to /sign-in. No redirect loop. No console flood of AuthApiError.
4. Click 'Continue with Google', complete the OAuth flow with a valid @agenticdream.com account.
5. EXPECT: land on /dashboard with an authenticated session. Server logs show no refresh_token_not_found errors.
6. Repeat step 1-3 with cookies fully deleted (first-visit simulation). EXPECT: same clean redirect, no errors.

No AC failures.

title: Fix 504 timeout on analytics generation in get-call route
description:
## User Story
As a call reviewer, I want analytics generation to complete without timing out, so that I can see interview results without a 504 error.

## Why
The LLM call in the analytics pipeline has no explicit timeout. The heavy model (openai.gpt-oss-safeguard-120b, 16k tokens) sometimes takes longer than the current implicit limit, producing a 504.

Failing call: call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm

## What to Change
Set the fetch timeout on the LLM call to **60 seconds**.

- File: src/lib/llm-client.ts — add an AbortController with a 60s timeout to the fetch call in callLlmEdgeFunction()
- Also add export const maxDuration = 60 to src/app/api/get-call/route.ts so Vercel allows the full 60s

No timeout-guard wrapping, no graceful degradation fallback — just raise the timeout to 60s.

## Edge Cases
- AbortController must be cleaned up (clearTimeout) whether the fetch succeeds or fails
- Existing error handling in callLlmEdgeFunction must still propagate non-timeout errors normally

## How to Verify
1. POST /api/get-call with call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm — should return 200 with analytics populated
2. No 504 in response
3. Confirm the fetch timeout is set to 60000ms in llm-client.ts

comments.author: pm
comments.text: GROOMED: Add maxDuration=300 to get-call route and wrap generateInterviewAnalytics in a 25s timeout guard with graceful degradation. User story: As a call reviewer, I want analytics generation to not cause 504s, so I can see call results. Edge cases: already-has-analytics path unaffected; malformed JSON handling preserved; timeout must not persist partial analytics; retry-analysis cron must still heal calls with null analytics. How to verify: POST get-call with call_1778195014547_42a9wgrdm returns 200 (not 504); retry-analysis cron later populates analytics; unit test for timeout-guard path (mock 30s delay, assert 200 + null analytics within 26s).

comments.author: eng1

title: Dark theme: MUI BarChart and LineChart axis labels black in dark mode
description:
## Why
summaryInfo.tsx has a BarChart (line 822, score distribution) and a LineChart (line 920, score trends) with no sx prop. MUI charts default to black axis tick labels and legend text — invisible on a dark background.

## What to Change
File: src/components/dashboard/interview/summaryInfo.tsx

### BarChart (line 822)
Add an sx prop:
```jsx
sx={{
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tickLabel': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-line': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tick': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
}}
```

### LineChart (line 920)
Add an sx prop with the same axis overrides PLUS legend text (LineChart renders a legend):
```jsx
sx={{
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tickLabel': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-line': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsAxis-tick': { stroke: 'var(--border) !important' },
  '& .MuiChartsLegend-series text': { fill: 'var(--foreground) !important' },
}}
```

## Edge Cases
- Series colors (#4F46E5 indigo, #9ca3af gray) are data colors — leave them
- The two PieCharts already have the legend text fix from a previous bead — do not touch them
- No logic changes — sx prop additions only

## How to Verify
1. Enable dark mode, open the overall analysis view for an interview with response data
2. Score Distribution bar chart: x-axis labels (0-10, 10-20 etc) and y-axis labels are visible (not black)
3. Score Trends line chart: x-axis date labels, y-axis score labels, and legend (Average Score / Overall Average) are all visible

title: summaryInfo dark mode: candidate metrics labels, sentiment chart, top performers bg
description:
## User Story
As an admin in dark mode, I want the candidate metrics, candidate sentiment, and top performers sections to be fully readable so I can analyse data without squinting.

## Why
Three concrete issues remain in summaryInfo.tsx after the previous fix:
1. bg-indigo-50/bg-indigo-100 are light-mode-only backgrounds — they appear as bright white/light boxes in dark mode
2. MUI PieChart legend text has no dark mode color override — it renders black and disappears on a dark background
3. text-muted-foreground on metric card labels is too dim in dark mode — operator wants those labels clearly visible

## What to Change
File: src/components/dashboard/interview/summaryInfo.tsx

### Fix 1 — Metric card backgrounds (Candidate Metrics section, ~lines 455, 465)
- bg-indigo-100 → bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-950/40
These appear on the completion rate and average duration stat cards.

### Fix 2 — Top performer row background (Top Performers section, line 866)
- bg-indigo-50 → bg-indigo-50 dark:bg-indigo-950/30
Each performer row uses this as its card background.

### Fix 3 — MUI PieChart legend text (Candidate Sentiment section, lines 480-483)
The sx prop currently only sets fontSize. Add a fill/color override so the legend text is visible in dark mode:
```js
sx={{
  "& .MuiChartsLegend-series text": {
    fontSize: "0.8rem !important",
    fill: "var(--foreground) !important",
  },
}}
```
Use the CSS variable --foreground so it adapts to both light and dark mode.

### Fix 4 — Metric label text visibility (Candidate Metrics section, lines 596, 601, 605, 610, 614, 617, 621, 626)
text-muted-foreground on metric labels ("Avg Answer Length", "Relevance Score", "Depth Score", "Consistency Score" and their unit spans like "words", "/10") is too dim in dark mode. Change label p tags to text-foreground and keep unit spans as text-muted-foreground so there is a visible hierarchy.

## Edge Cases
- Medal rank circles (bg-yellow-500, bg-gray-400, bg-orange-600 with text-white) — leave them, saturated colors work in both modes
- Indigo text (text-indigo-600) — leave it, reads well in dark
- PieChart data colors (#22c55e, #eab308, #eb4444) — leave them, these are chart slice colors not text
- No logic changes — styling only

## How to Verify
1. Enable dark mode and open the summary/analysis view for an interview
2. Candidate metrics: all label text ("Avg Answer Length" etc) clearly readable
3. Candidate sentiment: pie chart legend text visible (not black on dark)
4. Top performers: performer rows have dark-aware background, names and scores readable

title: Fix post-test redirect: admin to all-responses, candidate to individual result
description:
## User Story
As an admin completing a test run, I want to be redirected to the interview's all-responses page, not my individual result, so I can immediately see how the test performed across all responses.

As a candidate completing a test, I want to be redirected to my individual result page after results are compiled, so I can review my performance.

## Why
Admins are currently redirected to `/result/[callId]` after completing a test — the same page candidates see. This is wrong because admins don't care about their own result; they care about the aggregate responses view for the interview. The current behavior wastes the admin's time and may cause confusion.

## What to Change
File: `src/components/call/index.tsx`, line 674

Current code:
```ts
window.location.href = `/result/${callId}`;
```

The `Call` component already receives `isTestResponse` (line 55) and `interview` props. When `isTestResponse === true` (admin test run), redirect to the interview's all-responses page (`/interviews/[interviewId]`) instead. When `isTestResponse === false` (candidate), keep redirecting to `/result/[callId]`.

Check that `interview.id` is the correct field for the interviewId — verify against the interview type.

## Edge Cases
- If `interview.id` is undefined, log a warning and fall back to `/result/${callId}` rather than redirecting to a broken URL
- The fix is scoped to the single redirect at line 674; do not touch the duplicate-check redirect at line 353 or the check-response redirect at line 477

## How to Verify
1. Log in as admin, open an interview, click 'Test' to run through the interview
2. After completion: should land on `/interviews/[interviewId]` (all-responses page), NOT `/result/[callId]`
3. Open the same interview link as a candidate (no auth / candidate session)
4. After completion: should land on `/result/[callId]`
5. Confirm no regressions on lines 353 and 477 redirects

title: Fix webpack ENOENT cache errors and remove mobile browser targets
description:
## User Story
As a developer, I want the Next.js dev server to serve static chunks correctly, so that the app loads in the browser without 404 errors.

## Why
The dev server compiles successfully but all static chunk requests (webpack.js, main-app.js, app-pages-internals.js, layout.css etc.) return 404. Root cause is a corrupted .next/cache/webpack directory — some cache files have a trailing underscore extension (.pack.gz_) indicating interrupted writes. Webpack cannot read these and throws ENOENT unhandledRejection errors, which causes the client bundle to fail to serve. Additionally, the browserslist data is 15 months out of date and the build currently targets mobile browsers unnecessarily, adding compilation overhead.

## What to change
1. Delete the entire .next directory at C:\agentic-dream\internal-tools\agentfoloup\.next to wipe the corrupted cache and force a clean recompile.
2. Add a browserslist config to package.json that targets only modern desktop/evergreen browsers and explicitly excludes mobile. Use this:
   {
     "production": ["chrome >= 90", "firefox >= 90", "edge >= 90", "safari >= 14"],
     "development": ["last 1 chrome version", "last 1 firefox version", "last 1 safari version"]
   }
3. Run npx update-browserslist-db@latest to refresh the caniuse-lite data.
4. Restart the dev server and confirm all chunks load (no 404s, no ENOENT errors).

## Edge cases
- Do not delete node_modules or .env files — only .next.
- The browserslist production targets must not include any android, ios_saf, op_mini, or IE entries.
- If the dev server still shows 404s after clearing .next, check whether next dev is being invoked from the wrong working directory.

## How to verify
- Run npm run dev (or yarn dev) from C:\agentic-dream\internal-tools\agentfoloup
- Navigate to http://localhost:3001 — the page must load without browser console errors
- Dev server logs must show no ENOENT unhandledRejection errors
- Dev server logs must show no 404s for /_next/static/chunks/ paths
- The browserslist warning about stale data must be gone
close_reason: Fix complete — .next cleared, browserslist config added, build clean.

title: Fix: Array buffer allocation failed during Next.js webpack compilation
description:
## User Story
As a developer on Windows, I want the Next.js dev server to compile all pages including /sign-in without crashing, so that I can iterate on the app without restarting the server.

## Why
The dev server throws RangeError: Array buffer allocation failed during webpack zlib Gunzip on warm recompile of /sign-in/[[...sign-in]]. This crashes the Node.js process entirely, requiring a manual restart that may recur immediately on the next recompile. The crash makes the dev environment unreliable for the whole team. The project has a large dependency surface including @vladmandic/face-api (TensorFlow.js + model weights), pdfjs-dist, and langchain. Face detection was removed from the UI in commit 44a9af4 but @vladmandic/face-api was not removed from package.json — it is not imported anywhere in src/ and is a dead dependency. Webpack still resolves and attempts to process it during bundling, which is the leading hypothesis for why memory pressure tips over on warm recompile.

## What to Change
The desired end state (how to get there is eng's call):

1. npm run dev on Windows 11 compiles /sign-in/[[...sign-in]] and all other routes without RangeError on cold start AND warm recompile.
2. The fix must be committed to the repo — not a per-machine workaround. Any developer who clones and runs npm run dev gets the fixed behavior without extra setup.
3. npm run build completes without allocation errors.

Confirmed facts eng should know before starting:
- @vladmandic/face-api is in package.json but has zero imports in src/ (dead dep, highest-priority hypothesis)
- The current dev script is "next dev" with no NODE_OPTIONS heap flag
- next.config.js has no webpack chunk splitting configured
- pdfjs-dist, langchain, and @deepgram/sdk are also large but actively imported

## Edge Cases
- The crash is non-deterministic on cold compile but reliably hits on warm recompile (triggered by any file save).
- Specific to Windows 11 with default Node.js heap; Linux/macOS may not reproduce without memory constraints.
- If @vladmandic/face-api is removed: verify with grep that no file in src/ still imports it before closing — a missed import reinstates the crash.
- If NODE_OPTIONS is added to the dev script in package.json: confirm it does not break npm run build or npm run start.
- Docker build (Dockerfile is present): if the build stage runs next build inside a memory-constrained container, the same fix may be needed there too.
- If chunk splitting is chosen as the solution: verify /sign-in still hydrates correctly and the Clerk auth flow completes (sign-in and sign-up must work end to end).
- Webpack cache: a corrupted .next cache can mask or amplify the issue. If the fix is applied but the crash persists, clearing .next and node_modules/.cache is a prerequisite before declaring the fix ineffective.

## How to Verify
All steps must pass on a Windows 11 machine with the committed fix applied from a fresh install (no manual env vars, no local .next cache from before the fix):

1. Run npm run dev — server starts without error.
2. Navigate to /sign-in — page loads, no RangeError in terminal.
3. Edit any file in src/ three times in succession — each recompile completes without RangeError.
4. Run npm run build — exits 0 with no allocation errors.
5. If @vladmandic/face-api was removed: grep src/ for vladmandic — must return no matches.
6. If NODE_OPTIONS was added to package.json dev script: confirm the flag is present in the script definition, not only in .env.
close_reason: QA passed: dead dependency removed, zero vladmandic imports in src/, dev server starts clean, build compilation clean with no allocation errors.

This library bundles TensorFlow.js model weights and is one of the largest deps in the project — almost certainly the primary driver of the allocation failure. title: Fix unpadded base64url in invitations-get JWT verification
description:
## Why
The JWT verifier in invitations-get calls atob() on base64url-encoded strings after replacing - and _ characters, but does NOT add the required = padding. base64url strings omit trailing padding by design; atob() requires proper padding and will throw on unpadded input in some runtimes (including Deno). This will cause JWT verification to throw and the catch block to return false → every request gets a 401.

## What to Change
File: supabase/functions/invitations-get/index.ts

In the verifySupabaseJWT function, fix both atob() calls to add padding before decoding:

1. Payload decode (line ~18):
   Replace: JSON.parse(atob(payload.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/')))
   With: a helper that pads the string to a multiple of 4 before passing to atob()

2. Signature decode (line ~29-32):
   Same fix — pad before atob()

A safe padding helper:
  function b64urlDecode(s: string): string {
    s = s.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/');
    while (s.length % 4) s += '=';
    return atob(s);
  }

Use this helper for both the payload JSON.parse and the Uint8Array.from calls.

## Edge Cases
- JWT with no padding chars (standard JWTs always omit = padding in base64url)
- JWT where payload/signature length is already a multiple of 4

## How to Verify
1. Call invitations-get with a valid Supabase anon key JWT → 200 (was silently returning 401 before due to the throw)
2. Call with garbage token → 401
3. Call with no Authorization header → 401

comments.text: PLAN: Add b64urlDecode helper that pads base64url string to multiple of 4 before atob(). Replace both raw atob() calls in verifySupabaseJWT — payload JSON.parse and Uint8Array.from signature — with the helper. One-line fix, no logic change. File: supabase/functions/invitations-get/index.ts

title: Add JWT auth to invitations-get edge function
description:
## Why
invitations-get currently has no auth check, meaning anyone who knows a candidate's invitation UUID or application_id can retrieve their invitation record (including candidate_email and candidate_name). It should require a valid Supabase JWT.

## User Story
As a system owner, I want invitations-get to verify the caller's JWT, so that invitation data (including candidate PII) is not accessible to unauthenticated requests.

## What to Change

File: supabase/functions/invitations-get/index.ts

Add JWT verification using verify_jwt (Supabase edge function config) OR inline via supabase.auth.getUser():
- Check the Authorization header for a Bearer token
- Call supabase.auth.getUser(token) to verify the JWT
- If missing or invalid, return 401 Unauthorized

Note: The invitations-get function is called from two places:
1. The resolver page /call/[id]/[applicationId]/page.tsx (client-side, via /api/fn proxy)
2. The runner page /call/[id]/page.tsx (client-side, via /api/fn proxy)

The /api/fn/[...path]/route.ts proxy currently passes NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as the Bearer token. Verify whether the anon key is sufficient to pass supabase.auth.getUser(), or if the proxy needs to forward the user session token instead. If the anon key is insufficient, the proxy must be updated to pass through the original Authorization header from the browser, or use the service role for this specific function call.

Whichever approach works, ensure the resolver and runner pages still function correctly end-to-end after the change.

## Edge Cases
- No Authorization header → 401
- Invalid/expired JWT → 401
- Valid anon key (if supabase supports it) → 200

## How to Verify
1. Call invitations-get with no Authorization header → 401
2. Call invitations-get with a garbage token → 401
3. The resolver page /call/[jobId]/[applicationId] still resolves and redirects correctly
4. The runner page /call/[invitationId] still loads the interview correctly

Extract Bearer token from Authorization header. 2. Verify JWT signature with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET using Deno Web Crypto (HMAC-SHA256). 3. Check exp claim for expiry. Return 401 for missing/invalid/expired token. Proxy does NOT need updating — anon key passes signature verification. Files: supabase/functions/invitations-get/index.ts. title: Align invitations-create edge function with test-gorilla pattern
description:
## Why
The agentfoloup invitations-create edge function is missing three guards that exist in the test-gorilla equivalent. These gaps mean: (1) only DreamIT can call it — no admin fallback, (2) a re-sent invitation silently overwrites the existing one instead of surfacing a conflict, (3) an interview in a non-published/inactive state can still receive invitations.

## User Story
As a DreamIT integration or admin user, I want invitation creation to be consistent with the test-gorilla pattern, so that behaviour is predictable and safe across both products.

## What to Change

File: supabase/functions/invitations-create/index.ts

### 1. Dual auth (DreamIT secret OR admin Supabase JWT)
Current: only accepts DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET bearer token.
Change: extract auth into a helper (mirror test-gorilla's authenticate function):
- If bearer === DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET → allow
- Else verify as Supabase JWT via supabase.auth.getUser(), then check payload.role === 'admin'
- Reject with 401/403 otherwise

### 2. Reject duplicate application_id with 409 (no upsert)
Current: uses .upsert() with onConflict: 'application_id' — silently overwrites.
Change: switch to .insert() and catch Postgres unique violation code '23505', return 409 with message 'An invitation already exists for this application_id'.

### 3. Published/active status check on the interview
Current: creates invitation regardless of interview status.
Change: when fetching from interview_job, also select the interview status field (check what column name holds status in the interviews table — likely interviews.status or similar). If the interview is not in an active/published state, return 400 'Interview is not active'.
First read the interviews table schema to confirm the status column name and valid values before implementing.

### 4. Explicit POST-only method guard
Current: accepts any HTTP method.
Change: after OPTIONS handling, return 405 if req.method !== 'POST'.

### 5. Shared helpers (if available)
Test-gorilla uses _shared/cors.ts and _shared/auth.ts. Check if agentfoloup has equivalent shared helpers in supabase/functions/_shared/. If yes, use them. If not, inline the logic (do not create new shared files just for this task).

## Edge Cases
- Bearer token missing → 401
- Bearer is a JWT but user not found → 401
- Bearer is a JWT but role !== admin → 403
- application_id already exists in invitations → 409
- job_id not found in interview_job → 404 (already handled)
- interview not active/published → 400
- Non-POST method → 405

## How to Verify
1. POST with valid DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET bearer → 201 with invitation
2. POST with valid admin JWT bearer → 201 with invitation
3. POST with invalid/missing bearer → 401
4. POST with valid JWT but non-admin role → 403
5. POST same application_id twice → second call returns 409
6. POST for a job whose interview is not published → 400
7. DELETE/GET/PUT request → 405

title: Refactor call routing: /call/[jobId]/[applicationId] → redirect to /call/[invitationId]
description:
## Why
The current entry URL is /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId], which requires DreamIT to know the interviewId when generating candidate links. The desired design removes interviewId from the external link entirely — DreamIT only provides job_id and application_id, and the app resolves the rest internally.

## User Story
As a candidate receiving an interview link, I want to click /call/[jobId]/[applicationId] and be taken directly into my interview session, so that the link I receive is simple and does not expose internal interview template IDs.

## What to Change

### Step 1: New entry route /call/[jobId]/[applicationId]
Create: src/app/(user)/call/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx
- On load, call /api/fn/invitations-get?application_id={applicationId}
- If 404 → show 'No invitation found' error UI
- If expired → show 'Invitation expired' error UI
- If valid → redirect (router.replace) to /call/{invitation.id}
- Show a loading spinner while resolving

### Step 2: Repurpose /call/[invitationId] route
Modify: src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/page.tsx OR create a new route
The /call/[id] page needs to accept an invitation UUID, not an interview template ID.
- Fetch invitation by ID from the invitations table (add a new edge function invitations-get-by-id, or extend invitations-get to accept ?id= as well as ?application_id=)
- From invitation record, get interview_id (already stored in invitations table)
- Pass interview_id to the Call component as usual
- Also pass inviteId so is_started/is_submitted can be marked

### Step 3: Remove old route
Delete or deprecate: src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx
(The new two-route design replaces it)

### Step 4: Update middleware public routes
Ensure /call is still public in src/middleware.ts (it already is, so this may be a no-op).

## Edge Cases
- application_id not found in invitations table → show error, do not redirect
- invitation is expired → show error, do not redirect
- invitation already submitted → redirect to /result/{call_id} (check /api/check-response first, same as current logic)
- invalid UUID passed to /call/[invitationId] → invitation fetch returns 404, show error

## How to Verify
1. Visit /call/[validJobId]/[validApplicationId] → should redirect to /call/[invitationId]
2. Visit /call/[invitationId] directly → interview loads and starts
3. Visit /call/[badApplicationId] → shows 'No invitation found'
4. Visit /call/[expiredApplicationId] → shows 'Invitation expired'
5. Visit /call/[applicationId for already-submitted] → redirects to /result/[callId]
6. The Call component still marks is_started and is_submitted correctly via PATCH /api/invitations/[id]

title: Add verify_jwt=false and redeploy invitation edge functions
description:
## Why This Exists

invitations-create and invitations-get edge functions were deployed without disabling Supabase JWT verification. Supabase blocks requests at the gateway before the function code runs, so invitations-create rejects DreamIT's DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET bearer token with UNAUTHORIZED_INVALID_JWT_FORMAT, and invitations-get is unreachable without a valid Supabase JWT.

The existing edge functions in this project (retry-dreamit, retry-analysis, bamboo-sync) already use verify_jwt = false in supabase/config.toml — the new functions must follow the same pattern.

## What to Change

### 1. supabase/config.toml

Add entries for both new functions (place alongside the existing function blocks):

```toml
[functions.invitations-create]
verify_jwt = false

[functions.invitations-get]
verify_jwt = false
```

### 2. Redeploy both functions

```bash
npx supabase functions deploy invitations-create --project-ref murfeotrigphabsklxjw --no-verify-jwt
npx supabase functions deploy invitations-get --project-ref murfeotrigphabsklxjw --no-verify-jwt
```

Both commands must succeed (exit 0) with no errors.

## How to Verify

1. Call invitations-get with a dummy application_id — should return 404 { error: 'not_found' }, not UNAUTHORIZED:
   curl 'https://murfeotrigphabsklxjw.supabase.co/functions/v1/invitations-get?application_id=test-verify' -H 'apikey: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Im11cmZlb3RyaWdwaGFic2tseGp3Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NjI0NTU3MDYsImV4cCI6MjA3ODAzMTcwNn0.MeDowz-4dAvfT2IQjgXDJ8EAu-X3gGHovOqkN0Gnc9I'

2. Call invitations-create with the DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET and a real job_id — should return 201 with invitation object, not UNAUTHORIZED:
   curl -X POST 'https://murfeotrigphabsklxjw.supabase.co/functions/v1/invitations-create' -H 'Authorization: Bearer 55df947d-9765-44b5-8f7a-13789f75cf30' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"job_id": 124, "application_id": "TEST-APP-001", "candidate_email": "candidate@test.com", "candidate_name": "Test Candidate", "expires_hours": 48}'

## Working Directory

title: Invitation gate: DreamIT creates invitations via edge function, call route validates before interview
description:
## Why This Exists

Currently the existing /call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId] route starts an interview immediately with no gate. This feature adds:

1. An invitations table that DreamIT populates (via a dedicated edge function) before sending a candidate link
2. A gate on the existing call route: check the invitation on arrival, show an error if none exists or it has expired

This is purely additive — the existing call flow is unchanged for valid invitations.

## User Story

As DreamIT, I want to call an edge function to register a candidate invitation before sending them the interview link, so the system knows who is expected and when their invite expires.

As a candidate, I want to see a clear message if my link has expired or has no invitation, so I know to contact the hiring team.

## What to Change

### 1. Database Migration

Create invitations table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE invitations (
  id              uuid        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  interview_id    text        NOT NULL REFERENCES interview(id),
  application_id  text        NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  job_id          integer,
  candidate_email text        NOT NULL,
  candidate_name  text,
  expires_at      timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT (now() + interval '48 hours'),
  is_started      boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  is_submitted    boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at      timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at      timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_invitations_interview_id    ON invitations(interview_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_invitations_application_id  ON invitations(application_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_invitations_candidate_email ON invitations(candidate_email);
```

RLS: Disable (use service role key, same as all other tables in this project).

### 2. Supabase Edge Function: invitations-create

File: supabase/functions/invitations-create/index.ts

This is the endpoint DreamIT calls to register a candidate invitation.

- Auth: Bearer token must equal Deno.env.get("DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET"). Return 401 if missing or wrong.
- Method: POST
- Body:
  ```json
  {
    "job_id": 123,
    "application_id": "APP_456",
    "candidate_email": "jane@example.com",
    "candidate_name": "Jane Smith",
    "expires_hours": 48
  }
  ```
- Logic:
  1. Validate required fields: job_id, application_id, candidate_email
  2. Look up interview_id from interview_job table where job_id = body.job_id (take the first match)
  3. If no interview found for that job_id: return 404 { error: "no_interview_for_job" }
  4. Calculate expires_at = now() + (expires_hours ?? 48) hours
  5. Upsert into invitations (ON CONFLICT application_id DO UPDATE: set expires_at, candidate_email, candidate_name, is_started=false, is_submitted=false, updated_at=now())
  6. Return 201 with { invitation }

Follow the existing edge function pattern in supabase/functions/ (Deno, CORS headers, service role key from Deno.env).

### 3. Supabase Edge Function: invitations-get

File: supabase/functions/invitations-get/index.ts

- Public endpoint, no auth required
- Method: GET
- Query param: ?application_id={text}
- Returns invitation row with computed is_expired field (expires_at < now())
- Returns 404 { error: "not_found" } if no row matches
- Returns 200 { invitation, is_expired } if found

Follow the existing edge function pattern in supabase/functions/.

### 4. API Route: PATCH /api/invitations/[id]

File: src/app/api/invitations/[id]/route.ts

- No auth required (called server-side on behalf of candidate)
- Accepts: { is_started?: boolean, is_submitted?: boolean }
- Only allows setting flags to true (ignores attempts to set to false)
- Returns updated invitation

### 5. Modify Existing Candidate Call Route

File: src/app/(user)/call/[interviewId]/[jobId]/[applicationId]/page.tsx

Before any existing logic, add an invitation gate:

1. Extract applicationId from route params
2. Fetch invitation via the existing /api/fn/[...path] proxy: GET /api/fn/invitations-get?application_id={applicationId}
   - IMPORTANT: Check whether /api/fn/[...path]/route.ts already exists before creating it. If it exists, do NOT recreate it.
3. Evaluate the result:
   - **404 / not_found**: Render full-page error — "No invitation found. Please contact the hiring team."
   - **is_expired=true**: Render full-page error — "This invitation has expired. Please contact the hiring team."
   - **Valid invitation**: Proceed with existing interview logic exactly as before. Fire-and-forget: PATCH /api/invitations/{invitation.id} with { is_started: true }.

Style error states consistently with the existing (user) route UI. Use existing shadcn/ui components. Do NOT add email verification.

### 6. Mark Submission When Call Ends

When a response is marked is_ended=true (call completed), set invitations.is_submitted=true for the matching application_id. Find where is_ended is set on responses (likely a PUT or PATCH in /api/responses) and add:

```typescript
if (application_id) {
  await supabaseAdmin
    .from('invitations')
    .update({ is_submitted: true, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .eq('application_id', application_id)
}
```

### 7. Middleware Check

Verify /call/* is in the public routes list in src/middleware.ts. Do not remove it.

## What NOT to Build

- No /invite/* routes
- No /candidate/* pages
- No email verification step
- No new Next.js pages beyond modifying the existing call page

## Edge Cases

- job_id not found in interview_job table: invitations-create returns 404
- application_id conflict: upsert resets is_started and is_submitted to false and refreshes expiry
- is_submitted=true on arrival: do not block (candidate may revisit result page)
- Responses without application_id: no invite check, unaffected

## How to Verify

1. Call POST to invitations-create edge function with Authorization: Bearer {DREAMIT_FOLOUP_SECRET}, body: { job_id, application_id, candidate_email }
2. Confirm invitation row created in DB
3. Visit /call/{interviewId}/{jobId}/{applicationId} — invite valid, interview loads normally
4. Set expires_at to past in DB, revisit — see "invitation has expired" error
5. Visit /call/{interviewId}/{jobId}/unknown-app-id — see "no invitation found" error
6. Complete interview — invitation is_submitted becomes true

## Working Directory

All work happens in C:\agentic-dream\internal-tools\agentfoloup\super. Do not change files outside this directory. Do not work in your own agent directory.

title: Sign-in page does not display auth_failed or OAuth errors to user
description:
## Why
Two failure paths redirect to /sign-in with error params that are never shown:
1. auth/callback/route.ts redirects to /sign-in?error=auth_failed on failed code exchange — but the sign-in page only checks for error=unauthorized
2. signInWithOAuth() in sign-in/page.tsx destructures error but never uses it — silent failure on OAuth init errors

## What to change
File: src/app/(client)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx

1. Read error=auth_failed from searchParams (alongside existing unauthorized check)
2. Display an appropriate message for auth_failed: e.g. 'Authentication failed. Please try again.'
3. In handleGoogleSignIn(): after const { error } = await signInWithOAuth(), if error is truthy, show an inline error message to the user (set an error state, render it in the UI)

## Edge cases
- No error param: no change, page renders normally
- error=unauthorized: existing behavior preserved
- error=auth_failed: new message shown
- OAuth init error (network, invalid config): shown inline

## How to verify
- Navigate to /sign-in?error=auth_failed
- EXPECT: visible error message on the page
- Simulate OAuth failure (disconnect network before clicking Continue with Google)
- EXPECT: inline error shown, loading spinner stops

comments.author: pm
comments.text:
GROOMED

User story: As a user who hits an authentication failure, I want to see a clear error message on the sign-in page, so that I know what went wrong and can try again without being confused by a silent failure.

Acceptance criteria:

1. auth_failed query param — src/app/(client)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx:
   - Read the error search param from the URL alongside the existing unauthorized check.
   - When error === 'auth_failed': display the message 'Authentication failed. Please try again.' (or equivalent user-friendly copy) prominently on the page.
   - The message must be visible without scrolling on a standard viewport.

2. Existing error=unauthorized handling:
   - Must not be changed or removed. Existing message and behavior are preserved exactly.

3. OAuth init error (handleGoogleSignIn):
   - The existing const { error } = await signInWithOAuth(...) destructuring already captures the error.
   - If error is truthy: set a local error state and render the error message inline (near the Google sign-in button). Stop any loading/spinner state.
   - Must not silently swallow the error.

4. No error param:
   - Page renders normally with no error message shown.

Edge cases:
- Unknown error param values (e.g. error=foo): ignored — no message shown for unrecognized values.
- Both error=auth_failed AND OAuth init error occurring on the same render: both messages should not conflict; OAuth inline error takes precedence since it is the most recent action.
- User dismisses or navigates away: error state clears naturally (component unmounts).
- error=auth_failed arrives via server-side redirect (from callback route): message must render on first paint, not require client-side JS hydration to show.

How to verify:
1. Navigate directly to /sign-in?error=auth_failed.
2. EXPECT: 'Authentication failed. Please try again.' (or agreed copy) is visible on the page without any user interaction.
3. Navigate to /sign-in?error=unauthorized.
4. EXPECT: existing unauthorized message still shows — no regression.
5. Navigate to /sign-in (no params).
6. EXPECT: no error message shown.
7. On the sign-in page, simulate an OAuth init failure (e.g. block the Supabase OAuth endpoint in DevTools → Network → block URL, then click 'Continue with Google').
8. EXPECT: inline error message appears near the button; loading spinner stops.

title: Dark theme infrastructure: theme toggle + suppressHydrationWarning on layouts
description:
## User Story
As a user, I want a toggle to switch between light and dark mode, so I can choose my preferred theme.

## Why
This is the prerequisite for all other dark theme beads. Without a working toggle and correct html tag setup, the dark class won't apply reliably.

## What to Change
1. Both layout files need suppressHydrationWarning on the html tag if missing:
   - src/app/(client)/layout.tsx
   - src/app/(user)/layout.tsx

2. Create a ThemeToggle component at src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx using next-themes useTheme hook. It should cycle between light/dark (a simple sun/moon icon button is sufficient).

3. Wire the ThemeToggle into the navbar (src/components/navbar.tsx) so admins can toggle. For the candidate layout, place it somewhere accessible (e.g. top-right corner of the user layout or call page header if one exists).

4. Update ThemeProvider in providers.tsx to add enableSystem={false} so it only uses light/dark, not system default (keeps behavior predictable).

## Edge Cases
- suppressHydrationWarning only on the html tag, not body
- Toggle should persist preference (next-themes handles this via localStorage automatically)

## How to Verify
1. Toggle button is visible in admin navbar
2. Clicking it switches the html class between light/dark
3. Preference persists on page refresh
4. No hydration mismatch errors in console

title: Dark theme for admin and candidate
description:
## Why
The app currently has no dark theme support despite the infrastructure being 90% ready (next-themes installed, ThemeProvider wrapping the app, CSS variables defined for dark mode, Tailwind darkMode set to class). Admins and candidates need a dark theme option for comfortable use in low-light environments.

## Scope
Both surfaces need dark theme:
- Admin panel: navbar, side menu, dashboard, interviews pages, modals, layouts
- Candidate experience: call/interview page, result page, user layout

## What's Already in Place
- next-themes ^0.2.1 installed
- ThemeProvider with attribute=class, defaultTheme=light wrapping the app (providers.tsx)
- Full dark CSS variables defined in globals.css (.dark class)
- Tailwind darkMode: ["class"] configured

## What Needs to Done
1. Theme toggle UI + html tag fixes (suppressHydrationWarning on both layouts)
2. Shared components (navbar, sideMenu) — replace hardcoded bg-slate-100, text-black
3. Admin pages — replace hardcoded bg-white in layouts, toaster, interviews pages, modals
4. Candidate pages — replace hardcoded bg-white in call component, result page, user layout toaster
5. Auth pages — sign-in hardcoded bg-white, text-gray-*

## How to Verify
Admin: toggle dark mode → all admin pages render correctly with no white flash or unreadable text
Candidate: dark mode persists across call and result pages

title: Fix fullscreen not entering properly when candidate starts test
description: When a candidate navigates to the test page and starts the interview, the app is supposed to enter fullscreen mode. The fullscreen request lives in tabSwitchPrevention.tsx (requestFullscreen on the documentElement when isStarted fires). Candidates report fullscreen is not being triggered or is silently failing. Investigate why requestFullscreen() is not working on test start and fix it so the candidate reliably enters fullscreen when the interview begins.
notes:
**User Story**
As a candidate, I want the browser to automatically enter fullscreen when I click 'Start Interview' on the test page, so that the proctoring system can accurately track fullscreen exits during my assessment.

**Why**
Fullscreen never triggers because browsers block programmatic requestFullscreen() calls that happen outside a synchronous user gesture. The current code fires the request from a useEffect (tabSwitchPrevention.tsx line 51) that runs after React's render cycle, which is outside the gesture window. If not fixed, fullscreen_exit_count will never increment in test responses, making the fullscreen proctoring feature non-functional for all candidates.

**What to change**
Root cause: startConversation in src/components/call/index.tsx (line 468) is an async function that awaits multiple network calls before calling setIsStarted(true) at line 582. The useEffect in src/components/call/tabSwitchPrevention.tsx (lines 48-52) that calls document.documentElement.requestFullscreen() fires after the React render triggered by that state update — long after the button-click gesture context has expired. Browsers require requestFullscreen() to be called synchronously within the same call stack as the user gesture.

Fix:
- src/components/call/index.tsx ~line 468: add document.documentElement.requestFullscreen().catch(() => {}) as the very first statement of startConversation (before any awaits), wrapped in: if (typeof document !== 'undefined' && document.fullscreenEnabled && !document.fullscreenElement).
- src/components/call/tabSwitchPrevention.tsx line 51: remove the requestFullscreen() call from the useEffect body. Keep the cleanup return that calls exitFullscreen (lines 53-58) — that still needs to run when isActive becomes false.

**Edge cases**
- Safari on macOS has limited fullscreen API support; the existing catch(() => {}) handles silent failures. No additional handling needed.
- If the user's browser has blocked fullscreen permissions, the request fails silently and the interview still starts normally.
- The test page renders Call with isTestResponse={true} (src/app/(client)/interviews/[interviewId]/test/page.tsx line 124) — this prop does not affect the proctoring hook, so the fix applies uniformly to both test and live interview flows.
- If the interview reconnects to an existing call (reuseCallId path), calling requestFullscreen at the top of startConversation is harmless — it's a no-op if fullscreen is already active or not supported.
- The handleUnderstand callback in tabSwitchPrevention.tsx (line 42) already re-requests fullscreen after the candidate dismisses a proctoring warning dialog. This path is unchanged.

**How to verify**
1. Open the test page for any interview (src/app/(client)/interviews/[interviewId]/test/page.tsx).
2. Click 'Start Interview'.
3. The browser must enter fullscreen mode immediately — no second user action required.
4. Press Escape to exit fullscreen. The 'Integrity Warning' dialog must appear.
5. Click 'I understand' — the browser must re-enter fullscreen.
6. Complete and end the interview. Query the response row in Supabase: fullscreen_exit_count must be 1 (from the Esc press in step 4).

title: Remove face/multi-face detection saving from backend and frontend
description: The app currently tracks no_face_count, multiple_faces_count, and proctoring_events (no_face, multiple_faces types) in the database via the PATCH /api/responses/[callId] route. The operator wants this data never saved — strip it from the backend whitelist so the API rejects/ignores these fields, and remove the frontend hook call that sends it. This keeps the proctoring feature absent without leftover dead writes.
notes:
**User Story**
As an operator, I want face-detection data (no_face_count, multiple_faces_count, and no_face/multiple_faces proctoring events) never saved to the database, so that the feature is cleanly removed without dead writes polluting response rows.

**Why**
Face detection is being retired as a proctoring signal. Continuing to write these fields produces misleading data in the analytics pipeline and complicates future schema migrations that may want to drop the columns. If this bead is not shipped, every completed interview will continue recording unused face data that reviewers may misread as live signal.

**What to change**
1. src/app/api/responses/[callId]/route.ts line 32 — CANDIDATE_FIELDS currently includes 'no_face_count', 'multiple_faces_count', and 'proctoring_events'. Do NOT remove 'proctoring_events' from the whitelist — tab/window/fullscreen events still need to be saved. Remove only 'no_face_count' and 'multiple_faces_count' from CANDIDATE_FIELDS (and therefore ADMIN_FIELDS via the spread on line 35).

2. src/components/call/index.tsx lines 145-150 — Remove the useFaceDetection hook call and the faceDetectionDataRef ref plus its sync effect entirely.

3. src/components/call/index.tsx lines 639, 650-654 — Remove the faceDetection snapshot (line 639), no_face_count field (line 650), multiple_faces_count field (line 651), and the spread of faceDetection.events into the proctoring_events array (line 654). The proctoring_events array must still be sent with the remaining tab/window/fullscreen events.

4. src/types/response.ts lines 6-7 — Remove 'no_face' and 'multiple_faces' from the ProctoringEvent type union so the type reflects what can actually be stored.

5. src/types/response.ts lines 30-31 — Remove no_face_count: number | null and multiple_faces_count: number | null from the Response interface.

6. src/hooks/useFaceDetection.ts — The entire file is now unused; delete it.

**Edge cases**
- proctoring_events must stay in CANDIDATE_FIELDS; only the client-side array construction changes (no more no_face/multiple_faces entries spread into it).
- Existing database rows with non-null values in these columns are unaffected — no schema migration is required.
- After removing the hook call, grep for any remaining imports of useFaceDetection across the codebase.
- If noFaceCount or multipleFacesCount values are displayed anywhere in admin UI, those display components must also be updated to drop those metrics.

**How to verify**
1. Start an interview, sit with no face visible for >10 seconds, then end the interview.
2. Query the response row in Supabase for that call_id. Confirm: no_face_count is null (unchanged from insertion), multiple_faces_count is null, proctoring_events contains no entries with type 'no_face' or 'multiple_faces'.
3. Send a manual PATCH to /api/responses/<callId> with body { no_face_count: 5 } as the only field. Server must return 400 'No valid fields to update'.
4. TypeScript compilation passes with no errors after type removals.
5. No import of useFaceDetection remains anywhere in the codebase.

title: Remove verify_jwt=false from invitations-get edge function
description:
## User Story
As the platform, I want invitations-get to enforce JWT verification so that only authenticated users can fetch invitations.

## Why
invitations-get is called by the app (authenticated users), not by DreamIT or any external service. verify_jwt=false was incorrectly applied alongside invitations-create in commit 0ce330e, when the fix was only needed for the create function (which DreamIT calls without a JWT).

Leaving verify_jwt=false on invitations-get removes an auth check that should be there.

## What to change
In supabase/config.toml, remove the [functions.invitations-get] block that sets verify_jwt = false.
Keep [functions.invitations-create] verify_jwt = false as-is.

## Edge cases
- Confirm no other caller (DreamIT, cron, external) is calling invitations-get without a JWT
- After removal, authenticated app requests should still work (they pass a JWT)

## How to verify
- supabase/config.toml no longer has verify_jwt = false under [functions.invitations-get]
- Unauthenticated requests to invitations-get get a 401 (JWT required)
- Authenticated requests still succeed
close_reason: verify_jwt=false removed from invitations-get in commit 5699e7d. invitations-create unchanged.
